subtitle: Pattern Recognition in Dust Notes
location_text: The whole cast on one stage — first time the deck has rendered the ensemble together. Sunday.
format_note: I ↔ XX mirror: Magician's will → Judgement's awakening. Same gesture (the figure raising one arm) at two scales — Magician one-figure, Judgement all-figures. Also the deck's THIRD REFUSED card per Priestess journal — and unlike Justice, Judgement HOLDS the refusal. The chapter renders the ensemble without rendering the verdict.

ciphers.text: the whole cast in one frame — first time in vol5 the deck has rendered every named POV together
ciphers.encoding_hint: the classical Judgement card shows the dead rising at the angel's trumpet. Modern Mythology renders the same gathering without the calling — every named character stands in the frame because the chapter has reached the structural moment when they must all be visible together. The angel is offscreen; the trumpet is implicit. Nobody answers a call here; the chapter is the gathering itself.

ciphers.text: every figure on stage has one arm raised — the Magician's 'as above' gesture, multiplied across the ensemble
ciphers.encoding_hint: I ↔ XX mirror: the Magician's solo gesture rendered en masse. Modern Mythology refuses to depict a single judging figure — instead, the gesture that was Frasier's alone at I is now everyone's at XX. The classical sequence one → many is the deck's structural premise; Judgement is where the premise becomes diegetic.

ciphers.text: the chapter's own footer, faint: 'XX — third refused. Priestess: the deck refuses.'
ciphers.encoding_hint: the THIRD refused arcana per the Priestess journal (VI Lovers, XI Justice, XX Judgement). Justice flipped anyway; Lovers stays face-down; Judgement holds. The chapter renders the ENSEMBLE but refuses to render the VERDICT — Modern Mythology's Judgement is the gathering without the calling. The Priestess journal's annotation is the chapter's footer, visible by the player but not addressed by the figures on stage.

ciphers.text: small placards in front of each figure read DUST NOTES — Frank's notebook page from XIX, expanded across the ensemble
ciphers.encoding_hint: Sun (XIX) was Pattern Recognition in Dust Motes; Judgement (XX) is Pattern Recognition in Dust Notes. The placards are Frank's notebook page distributed — each figure carries the chapter's working method. The chapter's awakening is methodological: the cast does not rise from the dead, they rise from the noise into pattern.

ciphers.text: the central position on the stage is empty — no angel, no trumpet, no judge
ciphers.encoding_hint: the chapter's refusal is structural. The figure who would render the verdict is not in the frame; the position is preserved (the staging acknowledges the absence) but unfilled. The classical Judgement's angel is the chapter's specific refusal — the cast is gathered, the verdict isn't called. The deck has not appointed the figure who would do the calling.

hotspots.notes: DORMANT: appears only after the player has clicked the soldering iron on the Magician card. The 'as above, so below' gesture Frasier made alone at I is now visibly multiplied across the ensemble at XX. I ↔ XX mirror: one figure's will becomes the cast's awakening, same gesture at two scales. The classical sequence one → many is rendered diegetically; the cast is in the position the chapter requires them to be.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has read Frank's 'DUST NOTES' notebook title on the Sun card. The placards in front of each figure are now visibly Frank's notebook page — the title he wrote at XIX is the chapter's working method at XX. Sun seeded the title; Judgement distributes it. Pattern recognition expanded from one POV's working notebook to ensemble methodology.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has read the three-refused register on the Lovers card. A small upper-left annotation now reads 'XX — joins VI in the refused. JUSTICE moved.' The deck's relationship to its refusals is legible from XX's footer: Justice flipped, Judgement holds, Lovers holds. Two cards still face-down out of three; Judgement is in good refused company.

DORMANT: appears only after the Sun's four-motes-are-survivors hotspot fires. The empty centre position on the Judgement stage now visibly resolves: the figures in the gathering are the Sinkhole Nexus's survivors. The empty centre is the position the lost would have occupied. The chapter's refused verdict is the verdict on the absence — who was lost, by whose decision, with what responsibility — and the chapter HOLDS the refusal because the refusal is the only honest verdict the survivors can deliver. The ensemble does not need to render the verdict on the lost; the ensemble's existence is itself the verdict. They are here; the rest are not; the chapter is the chapter of the showing-up.

The gesture the cast holds (Magician's 'as above, so below' multiplied) is the gesture of fourteen survivors counting fourteen lost on each side of the body — one arm up for the living, one arm not-down-but-also-up for the dead. The deck's most precise tarot register: the survivors carry the lost in the same gesture. Alberto stands in the ensemble; he has begun to carry; the redemption arc's first chapter is the chapter of standing in the gathering and holding the gesture without naming what he is holding for.

→ wakes the Nexus thread further. → wakes content on World (XXI).

DORMANT: appears only after the Wheel's Alberto-is-son hotspot fires. The placards in front of each figure now visibly re-read as job titles, not just chapter names: 'COOK (D'Ambrosio diner)' under John, 'ENGINEER (D'Ambrosio industrial, former)' under Frasier, 'ARCHIVIST (D'Ambrosio household, former)' under Elicia, 'HOUSEKEEPER (D'Ambrosio household, 1985-2005)' under Marta (in absentia from Ward C), 'CONTRACTOR (D'Ambrosio render farm)' under Evangeline, 'OBSERVER (D'Ambrosio retainer, current)' under Frank, 'ESTABLISHMENT-OWNER (Antonio loan, forgiven)' under Daigle, 'HEIR' under Alberto, 'SISTER-IN-LAW' under Nicola, etc. The Judgement chapter's ensemble is also the D'Ambrosio labor census; the gathering is the workers' refusal to forgive on a schedule. The dancer who is not coming is the head of household; the workers are the ones who showed up. Alberto stands in the ensemble; the deck does not yet say what he asks them.

ENTRY HOTSPOT: clicking the empty center position dispatches daemon_listener.02 to fetch ENSEMBLE_01 (50:00, integrity 0.97 — refusal noted). The diorama renders the full recording as a scrubbable waveform; 15 event markers across 50 minutes. Five spoken lines (Frasier opens, Frank's trumpet, Maya quotes Quent, Dante names the dancer, the un-named figure on the river). Two Faith tail-thumps. Three Frog croaks (XXI oracle present at XX's edge). The chapter is the gathering held; daemon_listener.02's integrity report notes 'refusal noted' — the verdict-render is what the demon agreed not to surface.

DORMANT: appears only after the Priestess pomegranate-hour shelf has been unlocked. The card now visibly carries a small burgundy VHS-spine label in the corner, marked "XX — JUDGEMENT · 'the ensemble gesture'". Clicking it opens Elicia's relationship to this arcana as expressed in her episode for it. Elicia's relationship to ensemble is that she rejects solos. The Judgement episode is her gathering. She cast twelve people to rehearse one gesture — slow raising of the right hand to the level of the eye, held three seconds, lowered to the heart. The twelve people are the actual twelve survivors of the Sinkhole Nexus. She did not tell them they were survivors when she cast them. They learned by being cast. Frasier's comment under this episode ('the gesture is multiplied') is the deck's quietest confirmation. The deck's Judgement chapter is partly Elicia's casting record made public.

easter_eggs: Judgement is the FIRST chapter to render the whole ensemble in one frame. Up to XX, vol5 has been POV-by-POV; XX is the structural moment when the cast must be visible together. The chapter renders the gathering without rendering the calling.

the Magician's 'as above, so below' gesture is now everyone's. I ↔ XX mirror: one figure's will at I becomes the ensemble's awakening at XX. Same gesture, two scales — the classical sequence one → many becomes diegetic.

Judgement HOLDS the refusal. Justice (XI) flipped face-up anyway; Judgement does not. The chapter renders the ensemble but refuses to render the verdict. The angel position on the stage is empty by design.

Sun XIX → Judgement XX: 'Pattern Recognition in Dust Motes' → 'Pattern Recognition in Dust Notes.' One vowel apart, one chapter apart. Frank's notebook title from the Sun card is the placard each figure now carries.
the chapter is Sunday — closing the work-week ladder (Mon Chariot, Tue Temperance, Wed Devil, Thu Tower, Fri Moon, Sat Sun, Sun Judgement). Seven days, seven cards; the deck's structural week.

cross_references.weekday_chain_closes: chariot.json (Mon) through sun.json (Sat); judgement.json is Sun(day)
